sections.paragraph:
παρεκβαίνω (Hes.+; OGI 573, 17) lit. ‘to go beyond’ or ‘outside’ a boundary. In our lit. in transf. sense, to exceed limits of established authority go beyond, transgress τὶ someth. (EpArist 112) overstep τὸν ὡρισμένον τῆς λειτουργίας κανόνα 1 Cl 41:1. Of the sea οὐ παρεκβαίνει τὰ περιτεθειμένα αὐτῇ κλεῖθρα 20:6.

Hes Hes , date uncertain, perh. before VI b.c.—List 5

OGI OGI = Orientis Graeci Inscriptiones Selectae—List 3

lit. lit. = literal(ly); literature (refererences to [scholarly] literature)

transf. transf. = transferred

someth. someth. = something

EpArist EpArist = Epistle of Aristeas, II b.c.—List 5

1 Cl 1 Cl = 1 Clement—List 1

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 774
